title: Lesson 37 — Taking It Back

words.definition: to publicly withdraw a former statement or belief
words.example: Under pressure, the scientist recanted his earlier claim.

words.definition: to withdraw a statement as untrue or unjustified
words.example: The journal retracted the study after the fraud was found.

words.definition: to formally give up a claim, right, or belief
words.example: He renounced his citizenship after decades abroad.

words.definition: to solemnly renounce a belief, cause, or claim
words.example: She publicly abjured the ideology of her youth.

words.definition: to refuse to accept or be associated with; to reject
words.example: The party repudiated its former leader's remarks.

words.definition: to deny responsibility for or connection with
words.example: The agency disavowed any knowledge of the operation.

words.definition: to promise to give up or renounce something
words.example: He forswore alcohol after the accident.

words.definition: to revoke, cancel, or repeal a law, order, or agreement
words.example: The board voted to rescind the controversial policy.

words.definition: to officially cancel a decision, license, or agreement
words.example: Officials revoked the restaurant's permit after the violation.

words.definition: to repeal or cancel formally, especially a law or treaty
words.example: The new government moved to abrogate the old treaty.

words.definition: to make legally invalid or ineffective
words.example: A single loophole nullified the entire clause.

words.definition: a formal statement withdrawing a previous claim
words.example: The paper printed a retraction on the front page.

story: Under oath, the witness began to [[recant|recant]] everything he had told the press, forced to [[retract|retract]] each claim one by one. He seemed almost eager to [[renounce|renounce]] his old story, to [[abjure|abjure]] it completely, and to [[repudiate|repudiate]] the very sources who had once backed him. His lawyer urged him to [[disavow|disavow]] the leaked documents and [[forswear|forswear]] any future comment. The judge, for her part, moved to [[rescind|rescind]] the earlier ruling, [[revoke|revoke]] the witness's immunity, and effectively [[abrogate|abrogate]] the deal that had protected him, a decision that seemed to [[nullify|nullify]] the whole case. The next morning's headline was a single word: [[retraction|Retraction]].
